comparisons.difference: L0 exposes GetTemplatesForSmsAndEmail (profileId mandatory) and GetTemplates (profileId optional). GreenAI exposes GetForProfileAsync (profileId always from JWT claims). Same customer outcome: profile-scoped template listing. Clean rebuild.
comparisons.impact: No user impact — profile-scoped listing is the standard use case.

comparisons.difference: Both retrieve template by ID scoped to CustomerId + ProfileId. GreenAI additionally enforces channel consistency (INVALID_TEMPLATE_CHANNEL).
comparisons.impact: No user impact — GreenAI is functionally equivalent and more secure.

comparisons.difference: L0 InsertAsync creates SMS+email channel variants and seeds profile access in separate steps. GreenAI POST /api/v1/templates inserts template + seeds profile access rows in a single atomic transaction. Same customer outcome.
comparisons.impact: No user impact — GreenAI is functionally equivalent with stronger transactional guarantees.

comparisons.difference: L0 resolves template body/subject at send time and substitutes dynamic field tokens (from DynamicMergeFields table) before persisting payload. GreenAI resolves template + substitutes [FieldName] tokens (Name, Street, Number, PostalCode, City, Kvhx, Address) in SendDirect address mode using AddressOwnershipService data. Same customer outcome: merged payload in OutboundMessages. GreenAI is address-mode only in this slice; direct mode tokens remain unresolved.
comparisons.impact: Direct mode customers get unresolved tokens when using address-style template tokens. Address mode is fully equivalent.
comparisons.action: Extend token resolution to direct mode if per-recipient data becomes available.

comparisons.difference: L0 UpdateAsync modifies template name, body, subject, channel in separate steps with no profile-set replacement. GreenAI PUT /api/v1/templates/{id} updates all fields + replaces profile access set atomically in one transaction. Same customer outcome: template content updatable.
comparisons.impact: No user impact — GreenAI is functionally equivalent with stronger transactional guarantees and built-in profile set replacement.

comparisons.difference: L0 manages M:M profile-template access as standalone link/unlink operations. GreenAI seeds profile access at creation (CreateTemplate) and fully replaces the profile set on update (UpdateTemplate). The net business capability — any profile can be added or removed at any point — is fully covered through the update endpoint.
comparisons.impact: No user impact. Profile access is manageable via PUT /api/v1/templates/{id} ProfileIds array.
comparisons.action: None required. Dedicated standalone endpoint is not needed.

comparisons.difference: L0 DeleteAsync removes template and sub-entities. GreenAI DELETE /api/v1/templates/{id} hard-deletes template row and all MessageTemplateProfileAccess rows atomically in one transaction. Tenant guard (ExistsForCustomerAsync) ensures cross-customer protection.
comparisons.impact: No user impact — GreenAI is functionally equivalent.

comparisons.difference: L0 manages customer-specific DynamicMergeFields as a server-side CRUD table. GreenAI delivers the same business capability via a caller-supplied MergeFields dictionary on the send-direct request — no admin CRUD needed. Tokens resolved at send time via TemplateTokenMerger. Simpler design, same outcome.
comparisons.impact: No user impact. Callers supply any token values at send time. No predefined field registration required.
comparisons.action: None required. Server-side CRUD is over-engineering for the current capability set.

comparisons.difference: L0 has a separate email template entity type. GreenAI's unified template system covers both SMS (channel=1) and Email (channel=2) in the same create/update/delete endpoints. Email templates are first-class citizens: Subject is required for channel=2, NULL for channel=1. No separate entity type needed.
comparisons.impact: No user impact — GreenAI covers email templates natively via channel=2.
comparisons.action: None required. Unified channel model is cleaner than separate entity types.

summary: GreenAI covers list, get, create, update (atomic profile set replacement), delete (hard delete), profile access management (via update), runtime token merge for address mode, and caller-supplied dynamic merge fields on send-direct. Missing: email_template_crud (LOW — system notification emails, not customer templates). No CRITICAL or HIGH gaps. DFEP gate threshold achievable.
